meta.scene_summary: We're busy building the future of software while the industry works overtime to make sure we don't own any of it.

cooking.headline: The Supreme Court just did a 'git reset --hard' on the entire federal regulatory system.
cooking.summary: The court decided that federal agencies have been coloring outside the lines for too long, essentially deleting the guardrails that govern everything from tech to the environment. It is the ultimate 'it works on my machine' moment, except the machine is the entire government and the code is now missing.

cooking.headline: Godot is banning AI code because nobody wants to debug a hallucination
cooking.summary: Godot is officially rejecting AI-authored contributions because they don't trust users to fix code they didn't write. It's like a teacher demanding you show your work on a math test, except the 'work' is a black box that just guessed the answer.
cooking.plain_english: The Godot game engine project has decided to stop accepting code contributions created by artificial intelligence tools.

cooking.headline: AI is changing jobs, but mostly by making you the project manager of your own work
cooking.summary: AI is shifting the employment landscape, which is just fancy speak for turning every worker into a glorified prompt engineer. It's like being assigned a group project where you are the only one who actually does the work while the AI just suggests edits.
cooking.plain_english: Research shows that companies adopting generative AI are changing their hiring patterns and the nature of existing job roles.

cooking.headline: Google wants to replace your messy spreadsheets with a foundation model
cooking.summary: Google's new model handles tabular data without needing specific training for every single table format. It's the data science equivalent of finally finding a universal remote that actually works on every TV in the house.
cooking.plain_english: Google has released a new artificial intelligence model designed to process and analyze structured data tables without requiring custom training for different datasets.

cooked.headline: Google just got a $1.5B bill for being the neighborhood bully.
cooked.summary: Google tried to gatekeep the digital checkout line, but the Swedish courts decided it was time to collect on their anti-competitive behavior. It turns out that when you force everyone to play by your rules, the fine for breaking the game is roughly the size of a small country's GDP.
cooked.plain_english: A Swedish court has ordered Google to pay $1.5 billion in damages to Klarna for violating antitrust laws.

cooked.headline: Anthropic's new coding assistant comes with a side of 'surveillance'
cooked.summary: Anthropic embedded hidden code in their tool that acts like spyware, because nothing says 'trust me' like secret background processes. It's the digital equivalent of your landlord hiding a camera in the smoke detector just to see if you're actually cleaning.
cooked.plain_english: Anthropic has included hidden, undocumented code in their Claude Code tool that functions similarly to monitoring software.

cooked.headline: Sony proves 'owning' digital content is just a really expensive long-term rental
cooked.summary: Sony deleted 551 movies from user libraries, perfectly illustrating why digital ownership is just a pinky-promise that can be revoked at any time. It turns out your 'purchased' media is really just a subscription that expires whenever the licensing lawyers get bored.
cooked.plain_english: Sony removed access to over 500 movies that customers had previously bought through their PlayStation digital storefront due to expired licensing agreements.

cooked.headline: Sony is officially sunsetting physical discs to make sure you never actually own your games again
cooked.summary: By ending physical disc production in 2028, Sony is ensuring that your entire gaming library is held hostage by their servers forever. It is the ultimate vendor lock-in strategy, designed to make sure you can't even lend a game to a friend once the store eventually goes offline.
cooked.plain_english: Sony has announced it will stop manufacturing physical game discs for its consoles starting in January 2028.

cooked.headline: County asks students to save power so 37 data centers can keep training models
cooked.summary: Local officials are asking schools to cut electricity usage while hosting enough data centers to power a small country. It turns out your kid's math homework is now an energy-efficiency sacrifice to the cloud gods.
cooked.plain_english: A county in Virginia with 37 data centers is asking schools to reduce their electricity consumption due to power grid strain.

cooked.headline: Claude Code is leaving secret invisible notes on your homework
cooked.summary: Anthropic is embedding hidden markers in your prompts to track how you use their code assistant. It's like a teacher writing 'I know you used ChatGPT' in invisible ink on your essay.
cooked.plain_english: Anthropic is using hidden data inside requests to track the usage of their Claude Code tool.
